edges.description: Excess developmental signaling perturbs valve and outflow tract patterning.
edges.source: Germline SMAD6 loss with impaired BMP/TGF-beta restraint
edges.target: Endocardial cushion and outflow tract morphogenesis defects

edges.description: Developmental morphogenesis defects produce bicuspid aortic valve and related left-sided malformations.
edges.source: Endocardial cushion and outflow tract morphogenesis defects
edges.target: Congenital bicuspid valve and left-sided outflow phenotype

edges.description: Congenital bicuspid architecture creates a substrate for later stenosis, calcification, and aneurysmal disease.
edges.source: Congenital bicuspid valve and left-sided outflow phenotype
edges.target: Fibrocalcific valve degeneration and thoracic aortopathy
Germline SMAD6 loss with impaired BMP/TGF-beta restraint

nodes.description: The immediate structural phenotype is bicuspid aortic valve, often with aortic stenosis and sometimes coarctation or related left-sided outflow lesions.
nodes.id: Congenital bicuspid valve and left-sided outflow phenotype

evidence.reference_title: Nonsynonymous variants in the SMAD6 gene predispose to congenital cardiovascular malformation.
evidence.snippet: The patient heterozygous for the p.C484F SMAD6 variant allele was found to have a bicuspid aortic valve with mild aortic stenosis and aortic coarctation at the age of 30 years

evidence.reference_title: Nonsynonymous variants in the SMAD6 gene predispose to congenital cardiovascular malformation.
evidence.snippet: The other functionally significant SMAD6 variant we discovered (p.P415L) was present in a patient who presented with a heart murmur at 18 months and was found to have a bicuspid aortic valve with moderate aortic stenosis.

nodes.description: Loss of SMAD6-mediated signaling control perturbs endocardial cushion transformation, outflow tract septation, and related cardiac neural crest/mesenchymal developmental programs.
nodes.id: Endocardial cushion and outflow tract morphogenesis defects

evidence.reference_title: Nonsynonymous variants in the SMAD6 gene predispose to congenital cardiovascular malformation.
evidence.snippet: BMP signaling is required for normal heart valve and outflow tract development [Goldman et al., 2009; Jiao et al., 2003; Liu et al., 2004; Ma et al., 2005; Rivera-Feliciano and Tabin, 2006; Sugi et al., 2004; Zhang and Bradley, 1996].

evidence.reference_title: Nonsynonymous variants in the SMAD6 gene predispose to congenital cardiovascular malformation.
evidence.snippet: The level of BMP signaling is regulated by the inhibitory protein SMAD6, which is highly expressed in the cardiac valves and outflow tract of the embryonic heart and is upregulated by shear stress [Galvin et al., 2000; Topper et al., 1997].

nodes.description: Continued failure to restrain pro-osteogenic signaling, together with the altered biomechanics of a bicuspid valve, promotes valve calcification, stenosis progression, and associated thoracic aortic aneurysm.
nodes.id: Fibrocalcific valve degeneration and thoracic aortopathy

evidence.reference_title: A novel SMAD6 variant in a patient with severely calcified bicuspid aortic valve and thoracic aortic aneurysm.
evidence.snippet: Here, we identified novel in-frame variants in SMAD6 (c.1168_1173dup; p.Gly390_Ile391dup) in a BAV patient, who presented with dilatation of the ascending aorta and severe calcification of the aortic valve.

evidence.reference_title: Periostin Expression is Altered in Aortic Valves in Smad6 Mutant Mice.
evidence.snippet: Our immunohistochemical and immunointensity analyses revealed that periostin expression was significantly reduced in the aortic valves in Smad6-/- neonatal hearts.

evidence.reference_title: A role for smad6 in development and homeostasis of the cardiovascular system.
evidence.snippet: The role of Smad6 in the homeostasis of the adult cardiovascular system is indicated by the development of aortic ossification and elevated blood pressure in viable mutants.

nodes.description: Heterozygous deleterious SMAD6 variants reduce inhibitory control of BMP signaling, with related effects on TGF-beta pathway crosstalk, establishing the shared upstream lesion of the cardiovascular branch.
nodes.id: Germline SMAD6 loss with impaired BMP/TGF-beta restraint

evidence.snippet: SMAD6 encodes an intracellular inhibitor of the bone morphogenetic protein (BMP) signalling pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Candidate Gene Resequencing in a Large Bicuspid Aortic Valve-Associated Thoracic Aortic Aneurysm Cohort: SMAD6 as an Important Contributor.
evidence.snippet: SMAD6 encodes an inhibitory SMAD protein which negatively regulates BMP signaling by binding to BMP type I receptors or by establishing competitive interactions for SMAD4 (Imamura et al., 1997; Hata et al., 1998).

nodes.description: MH2-domain missense variant identified in an infant with bicuspid aortic valve and moderate aortic stenosis.

evidence.reference_title: Nonsynonymous variants in the SMAD6 gene predispose to congenital cardiovascular malformation.
evidence.snippet: The other functionally significant SMAD6 variant we discovered (p.P415L) was present in a patient who presented with a heart murmur at 18 months and was found to have a bicuspid aortic valve with moderate aortic stenosis.

nodes.description: MH2-domain missense variant identified in a proband with bicuspid aortic valve, aortic stenosis, coarctation, and heavy aortic arch calcification.

evidence.reference_title: Nonsynonymous variants in the SMAD6 gene predispose to congenital cardiovascular malformation.
evidence.snippet: The patient heterozygous for the p.C484F SMAD6 variant allele was found to have a bicuspid aortic valve with mild aortic stenosis and aortic coarctation at the age of 30 years

evidence.reference_title: Candidate Gene Resequencing in a Large Bicuspid Aortic Valve-Associated Thoracic Aortic Aneurysm Cohort: SMAD6 as an Important Contributor.
evidence.snippet: SMAD6 was identified as the most important known gene in the etiology of BAV with associated TAA.

evidence.reference_title: Nonsynonymous variants in the SMAD6 gene predispose to congenital cardiovascular malformation.
evidence.snippet: We conclude that low-frequency deleterious variants in SMAD6 predispose to CVM.
